criteria.name: Team Coverage
criteria.1: Fewer than 20 teams covered, or no explicit accounting of missing teams
criteria.3: 25-29 teams covered with top-3 RP per team, missing teams are flagged
criteria.5: All 30 MLB teams covered with top-3 RP per team (90 RP entries), every entry has a save_role_certainty score, per-team situation tag present

criteria.name: Source Quality and Freshness
criteria.1: Single source only (e.g., just RotoBaller) and stale (>5 days old), or no source URLs cited
criteria.3: RotoBaller pulled on the day of emission, one secondary source consulted for volatile situations, URLs cited
criteria.5: RotoBaller pulled same-day, Closer Monkey cross-referenced for all sub-70 certainty situations, Athlon Closer Confidential grades captured in April/May, every source URL cited with last-updated timestamp noted, stale pages downgraded in confidence

criteria.name: save_role_certainty Rubric Application
criteria.1: Scores assigned without using the 5-component rubric, or components applied inconsistently across teams
criteria.3: All 5 components (role label, recent performance, velocity, manager signal, contract/age) considered for most RPs, scoring mostly consistent
criteria.5: Every scored RP has all 5 components evaluated with cited evidence, scores rescaled correctly to 0-100, per-RP score_confidence attached (0.0-1.0), insufficient-data RPs explicitly marked rather than guessed

criteria.name: Committee Detection
criteria.1: Committees not flagged even when multiple arms are in the mix, or named 'closers' in committees score above 70
criteria.3: Committees flagged when top-2 are within 15 points, named closer in committee capped at 60-65
criteria.5: Committees flagged on both the 15-point spread criterion AND manager language criterion, platoon-handcuff analysis performed for speculation sizing, no RP on a committee team scores above 60, flag table includes all committee situations league-wide

criteria.name: Velocity Trend Analysis
criteria.1: Velocity not checked for any closer, or checked without comparing to baseline
criteria.3: Velocity checked for closers below 80 certainty, baseline comparison present for most of them
criteria.5: Velocity checked for every closer scoring below 80, 3-outing rolling average compared to 2026 season baseline on Baseball Savant, -1 mph sustained drop triggers score penalty, closer who has not thrown in 7+ days flagged explicitly

criteria.name: Speculation Target Identification and Bid Sizing
criteria.1: No speculation targets identified, or targets include clearly-owned handcuffs behind elite locked closers, or FAAB bids exceed $10 without category-strategist concurrence
criteria.3: Speculation targets correctly restricted to 30-60 certainty range with volatility on incumbent, FAAB bands applied mostly correctly
criteria.5: Speculation targets filtered on both save_role_certainty band AND incumbent volatility flags, ranked by speculation_score, Yahoo availability cross-referenced, FAAB bid bands applied per the guidance table, ceiling rule enforced ($10 max without category-strategist concurrence), floor rule enforced (min $1 on anything stash-worthy), platoon handcuff preferred in committees

criteria.name: Punt-SV Decision Logic
criteria.1: Punt-SV not considered even when trigger conditions are met, or punt-SV recommended without justification
criteria.3: Punt-SV trigger evaluated, recommendation issued when appropriate, basic justification provided
criteria.5: Punt-SV trigger formally evaluated against all three conditions (zero >=75 certainty RPs owned, 4+ slots would need speculation, SV losing/tied-low-reach per category-state), explicit recommendation to category-strategist with action ladder (DROP/HOLD/ADD), justification ties back to SV volatility being the league's most punt-worthy cat, review date set for next category-state read

criteria.name: Signal File Quality and Handoff
criteria.1: Signal file missing frontmatter fields, no source URLs, no confidence score, or not validated through mlb-signal-emitter
criteria.3: Signal file has required frontmatter, source URLs cited, confidence present, most required fields populated, validated
criteria.5: Signal file passes mlb-signal-emitter validation on first try, frontmatter includes teams_covered/rps_scored/committees_flagged/dfa_risks_flagged/punt_sv_recommended counts, depth charts + flat per-RP table + speculation table + committee/DFA flag table + punt-SV block all present, red-team findings noted for low-confidence entries, handoff targets (waiver-analyst, category-strategist, lineup-optimizer) can consume without re-derivation

key_requirements: Athlon Closer Confidential grades are freshest here — capture every team's letter grade
Spring training manager quotes are LESS reliable than in-season quotes — discount component 4 by half until 3 regular-season weeks have passed
Expect 6-10 committees in early April that will resolve by mid-May — flag all of them and revisit weekly
Velocity baseline is thin with only 5-10 outings; use prior-season velocity as supplementary baseline
DFA-risk is rare in April (rosters still set); set expectation to 0-1 league-wide
common_pitfalls: Scoring spring training 'named closer' as locked when no regular-season reps exist (cap at 75 until 10 regular-season appearances)
Ignoring off-season signings who displaced incumbents (check transaction log, not just current RotoBaller)
Bidding too aggressively on April committees before they resolve — hold FAAB for the resolution week

key_requirements: Roles are mostly settled — most teams should have a save_role_certainty 80+ closer by now
Committees that persist past May are structural (rebuilding teams, injury-ravaged bullpens) — not temporary
Trade-deadline positioning (mid-July) starts generating role-change rumors — add a 'trade rumor flag' to volatility assessment
Velocity trends are now reliable — 30+ outings of baseline available
common_pitfalls: Not accounting for deadline trades — a closer can change teams overnight and lose the role
Treating a 2-week slump as a role change when the manager hasn't signaled it

key_requirements: Every closer on a bottom-10 team is a deadline flight risk — cap their save_role_certainty at 75 regardless of performance (they may be traded to a setup role)
Every contender with a shaky closer may trade FOR one — flag the teams and the rumored names
Handcuffs on contenders become more valuable if the incumbent is traded to a new team
Check MLB trade rumor aggregators (MLBTradeRumors.com) alongside RotoBaller
common_pitfalls: Forgetting that an acquired closer displaces the incumbent on the new team (reducing save_role_certainty for BOTH the displaced arm and any prior handcuff)
Bidding high FAAB on handcuffs of teams likely to BUY a closer at the deadline

key_requirements: Closers on eliminated teams may be shut down for workload management — treat as injury-flag (velocity check mandatory)
Contenders may ride hot hands in high-leverage even over named closer — score volatility +5-10 points if the team is in a tight playoff race
This is our fantasy playoff window (weeks 21-23) — every save_role_certainty directly affects playoff outcomes
Punt-SV trigger gets re-evaluated weekly at this stage
common_pitfalls: Dropping a locked closer on an eliminated team (he still pitches the 9th until shutdown)
Not flagging 'setup men who got the 9th in a playoff game' — those are real vultures

common_failure_modes.symptom: Depth chart reflects last week's reality; a role change has already happened and is not captured
common_failure_modes.detection: Closer Monkey or Twitter/X beat writers show a role change that RotoBaller has not reflected; last-updated timestamp on RotoBaller > 48h old
common_failure_modes.fix: Downgrade run confidence to 0.6, elevate Closer Monkey to primary, manually correct the affected team's entry, note the lag in the signal file

common_failure_modes.symptom: A named closer on a team that RotoBaller or Closer Monkey labels 'Committee' scores 80+ despite the volatility
common_failure_modes.detection: Cross-check every closer scoring above 75 against committee flags in any source
common_failure_modes.fix: Cap committee-situation closers at 60. Volatility = low certainty, regardless of who got the last save.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Speculation bid recommended above $10 without documented category-strategist concurrence
common_failure_modes.detection: Every speculation target with a bid >$10 should have a concurrence note referencing the category-state signal
common_failure_modes.fix: Lower the bid to $10 ceiling or attach the category-strategist concurrence. If neither works, flag for user review.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Speculation target in a committee is the same-handedness arm as the incumbent, not the platoon handcuff
common_failure_modes.detection: In any committee, check the handedness of top-2 arms; if same-handed, check the third option — the opposite-handed arm may be the better speculation
common_failure_modes.fix: Re-sort committee speculation by upcoming 2-week 9th-inning LHB/RHB exposure. Recommend the platoon-advantaged arm.

common_failure_modes.failure: Missing velocity check on wobbly closers
common_failure_modes.symptom: A closer scores 65-75 with no velocity trend data cited
common_failure_modes.detection: Every closer < 80 certainty should have velocity data in the scoring evidence
common_failure_modes.fix: Pull Baseball Savant, compute 3-outing rolling average vs season baseline, re-score component 3.

common_failure_modes.symptom: User has zero elite closers, reliever pool is thin league-wide, but the signal file contains no punt-SV recommendation
common_failure_modes.detection: Check: if user-owned closers all < 75 AND 4+ RP slots would need specs to compete, punt-SV should fire
common_failure_modes.fix: Add the punt-SV block to the signal body and set punt_sv_recommended: true in frontmatter. Hand off to category-strategist.

common_failure_modes.failure: DFA-risk not flagged for demoted closer
common_failure_modes.symptom: An ex-closer scores below 25 but is not flagged for DROP
common_failure_modes.detection: Every RP scoring below 25 should be evaluated for DFA-risk
common_failure_modes.fix: Cross-reference options status and contract. If out of options or non-guaranteed deal, add DFA-risk flag and DROP recommendation.

common_failure_modes.symptom: Signal file uses jargon like 'handcuff,' 'vulture,' 'hold' without plain-English translation
common_failure_modes.detection: Review the final output as if you have never played fantasy baseball — any unexplained jargon is a failure
common_failure_modes.fix: Wrap all jargon in parentheses on first use. Example: 'speculation-worthy handcuff (the next-in-line reliever who would take the closer job if the current guy falters).'
